pi_items.source: Web search - MedlinePlus, Mayo Clinic, Cancer Research UK confirm Cabozantinib is a real tyrosine kinase inhibitor approved for treating medullary thyroid cancer, renal cell carcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, and other cancers

Web search - All three drugs confirmed as real medications: Cyclizine is an antihistamine/antiemetic; Loperamide is an antidiarrheal (actually recommended for managing Cabozantinib-induced diarrhea); Codeine is an opioid analgesic

Direct examination of actual app screenshot (results_key.png) from evidence_docs showing Liverpool Cancer iChart Results Key dialog. The dialog explicitly displays all five traffic light colors with their meanings: Red='Do not coadminister', Orange='Potential interaction requiring monitoring', Yellow='Potential weak interaction', Green='No significant interaction', Grey='No clear data'
Confirmed by task.json description (line 6) and setup_task.sh (line 8) - both reference this exact file path

pi_items.verified_value: Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive - developed by Radboudumc and University of Liverpool Drug Interactions Group
pi_items.source: Web search - cancer-druginteractions.org and Google Play/App Store confirm app is real, available on both Android and iOS platforms. Package name: com.liverpooluni.ichartoncology. The archived version uses interaction data last updated June 2022.

pi_items.verified_value: Real drug interaction data from Liverpool Drug Interactions Group
pi_items.source: Evidence file ibrutinib_comedication_contraindication_screen_evidence.json explicitly states: 'App contains real drug interaction data from Liverpool Drug Interactions Group'. Web search confirms this is a legitimate medical reference application used by healthcare professionals.

privileged_info_summary: Cabozantinib is a real tyrosine kinase inhibitor approved for treating multiple cancer types including renal cell carcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, and medullary thyroid cancer. The task uses the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive, a real drug interaction reference application developed by the University of Liverpool Drug Interactions Group, containing real interaction data last updated in June 2022. The three co-medications are real drugs: Cyclizine (antihistamine/antiemetic), Loperamide (antidiarrheal—actually recommended for Cabozantinib-induced diarrhea), and Codeine (opioid analgesic). The app uses a verified traffic light system with five colors: Red (do not coadminister), Orange (potential interaction requiring monitoring), Yellow (potential weak interaction), Green (no significant interaction), and Grey (no clear data).
